mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate reduces the equity discount rate and supports risk appetite, so lower policy rates should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the move is not offset by rising inflation.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX returns are not positive more often after FEDFUNDS declines than after increases, or if the signal disappears when CPIAUCSL is flat-to-falling.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals deteriorating labor income and weaker forward demand, so equity returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days after a clear unemployment break.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment breaks are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the effect vanishes when PAYEMS is also weakening.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates while HOUST remains subdued, the market reads resilient consumption with constrained housing supply, which supports near-term corporate revenue expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined RSAFS-up/HOUST-down condition does not outperform either series alone in explaining 20d SPX returns, or if positive returns are absent in the interaction sample.

mechanism_narrative: An advance in INDPRO after a prior downturn signals output normalization and better margins for cyclicals, so the S&P 500 should post positive returns over the next 60 trading days once industrial production turns up decisively.
failure_condition: Reject if industrial-production upturns are not followed by above-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the sign flips negative after the threshold is crossed.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in HOUST indicates stronger housing demand and spillover spending on durable goods and services, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if rising housing starts are followed by flat or negative 60d SPX returns in most instances, or if the effect is absent when RSAFS is also improving.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation is falling while FEDFUNDS stays high, real policy tightens and valuation multiples compress, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the disinflation-plus-restrictive-policy state is followed by positive 20d SPX returns more than 40% of the time, or if the interaction term is not negative in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: Stable PAYEMS with low UNRATE confirms a healthy labor market and supports earnings visibility, so the S&P 500 should rise over the next 5 trading days when labor conditions remain firm.
failure_condition: Reject if weeks with stable PAYEMS and low UNRATE do not produce positive 5d SPX returns more often than the unconditional baseline, or if the sign is persistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: Weak GDPC1 growth combined with soft RSAFS indicates slowing final demand and revenue deceleration, which should push S&P 500 returns negative over the next 60 trading days in an early slowdown regime.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous weakness in GDPC1 and RSAFS is not followed by negative 60d SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the joint signal is weaker than either series alone.
